EigenInference.claim: EigenInference is a macOS menu bar application that wraps the Rust darkbloom binary
The app follows MVVM architecture with StatusViewModel as central state manager
App switches between accessory and regular activation policies based on window visibility
StatusViewModel centralizes all provider state and coordinates between managers and UI
Status polling occurs every 5 seconds to check provider health
ProviderManager wraps the Rust binary as a subprocess with monitoring and auto-restart
Auto-restart functionality with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts
CLIRunner centralizes all darkbloom subcommand execution
Binary resolution follows consistent search order across app and CLI
ConfigManager handles shared provider.toml configuration between app and CLI
SecurityManager evaluates machine security posture for coordinator trust decisions
Trust level determination requires Secure Enclave, MDM enrollment, SIP, and Secure Boot
ModelManager discovers MLX models in HuggingFace cache directory
MenuBarView is the primary UI shown when clicking the menu bar icon
SetupWizardView provides comprehensive onboarding wizard for first-time users
DesignSystem provides unified colors, typography, and components matching brand guidelines
LaunchAgentManager handles macOS LaunchAgent for automatic app startup
TelemetryReporter ships crash reports and telemetry to coordinator endpoint
Application has zero external Swift package dependencies
Provider status polling checks process running state and backend health
Configuration synchronization writes schedule settings to provider TOML
Settings changes trigger automatic configuration updates via ConfigManager
Security verification runs parallel checks and determines trust level

EigenInferenceEnclave.claim: Swift package defines static library and CLI executable with EigenInferenceEnclaveCLI depending on EigenInferenceEnclave
SecureEnclaveIdentity manages hardware-bound P-256 signing keys with private key isolation in Secure Enclave
AttestationBlob contains comprehensive hardware/software security state with alphabetically ordered fields
FFI bridge uses @_cdecl for C-callable functions with Unmanaged.passRetained memory management
CLI tool creates ephemeral SecureEnclaveIdentity and AttestationService for each invocation
Attestation creation uses JSONEncoder with sortedKeys for deterministic JSON and signs with Secure Enclave
Signing operation happens inside Secure Enclave hardware and returns DER-encoded signature
System information helpers use system_profiler and sysctl for hardware identification
FFI signing function takes data pointer and length, returns base64-encoded DER signature
Full attestation FFI function accepts encryption key and binary hash parameters
C header defines opaque identity handle and memory management functions
Component imports CryptoKit and Foundation for cryptographic operations and system utilities
RDMA check uses rdma_ctl command to ensure remote memory access is disabled
Authenticated Root Volume check uses diskutil to verify sealed system volume

EigenInferenceEnclaveCLI.claim: CLI imports CryptoKit, EigenInferenceEnclave, and Foundation libraries and provides command-line tool for Secure Enclave attestation
printUsage function displays help text for attest and info commands with options documentation
cmdAttest function checks Secure Enclave availability, creates ephemeral identity, generates attestation, and outputs JSON
CLI creates SecureEnclaveIdentity and AttestationService instances from EigenInferenceEnclave dependency
cmdInfo function reports Secure Enclave availability and generates ephemeral public key information
Manual argument parsing handles attest command with --encryption-key and --binary-hash options
Command dispatch uses switch statement to route attest and info commands with error handling
JSONEncoder configured with ISO 8601 dates and sorted keys for deterministic output
Error handling outputs to stderr and exits with code 1 on failures
WebSocketBridge is empty stub indicating removed TLS bridge due to Apple keychain restrictions
Swift package defines eigeninference-enclave executable target with EigenInferenceEnclave dependency
Secure Enclave availability check using SecureEnclave.isAvailable with error exit if unavailable

analytics.claim: Main application orchestrates service initialization with configuration loading and error handling
Pluggable storage backends implemented via buildStore function supporting memory and PostgreSQL
Configuration struct defines environment-based settings for different deployment scenarios
Configuration validation ensures proper secret management for different backends
Service interface defines the contract between HTTP and business logic layers
Health endpoint provides service status with backend connectivity validation
Overview endpoint returns network-wide statistics via service layer
Earnings leaderboard endpoint with comprehensive parameter validation and error handling
CORS middleware provides configurable cross-origin support for browser integration
Store interface abstracts storage operations for pluggable backend implementations
Service struct coordinates between storage and pseudonym generation with dependency injection
EarningsLeaderboard method implements core business logic for data aggregation and formatting
MemoryStore includes hardcoded test data for development and testing purposes
PostgresStore implementation uses connection pooling for production database operations
PostgreSQL overview query uses complex aggregation with provider and earnings statistics
PostgreSQL leaderboard query implements dynamic SQL generation based on scope and window parameters
Pseudonym generator requires secret for deterministic but unpredictable alias creation
Alias generation uses HMAC-SHA256 with kind and stable ID to create adjective-animal-number format
PostgreSQL driver pgx/v5 version 5.8.0 is the primary external dependency
Transitive dependencies include connection pooling, synchronization, and text processing libraries

coordinator.claim: The coordinator serves as the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network, running in a GCP Confidential VM
Main function bootstraps core components including store, registry, and server configuration
Server struct contains all major subsystems including registry, store, billing, and rate limiters
Registry manages provider fleet with trust levels and round-robin routing
Store interface provides abstraction for API keys, usage tracking, and balance management
Billing service provides unified payment processing with Stripe integration
Consumer handlers implement OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoints with trust model
Provider WebSocket management handles registration, attestation, and inference relay
External dependencies include PostgreSQL driver, WebSocket library, Datadog clients, and crypto libraries
Routes method registers all HTTP endpoints for consumers, providers, and admin
Billing configuration supports Stripe payments with referral system
Datadog integration provides APM tracing and metrics collection
MDM integration enables independent provider security verification
Consumer request flow includes authentication, rate limiting, balance reservation, and streaming
Provider WebSocket connections handle registration and challenge-response verification

darkbloom.claim: Component is named 'darkbloom' version 0.4.7 and is described as 'EigenInference provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs'
Provider agent architecture includes hardware detection, model scanning, backend management, coordinator connection, private text engine, attestation, and crypto
CLI supports 18+ commands including init, serve, install, status, models, doctor, start, stop, update, login, logout, and others
WebSocket client manages provider connection to coordinator with automatic reconnection, registration, heartbeats, and inference request handling
Security hardening implements runtime protections including PT_DENY_ATTACH, SIP verification, and memory wiping to prevent inference data inspection
PT_DENY_ATTACH syscall prevents debugger attachment using ptrace with constant PT_DENY_ATTACH = 31 on macOS
In-process inference engine embeds Python via PyO3 for secure execution without IPC channels, protected by PT_DENY_ATTACH and Hardened Runtime
Ephemeral X25519 key pairs are generated fresh on every provider launch with secrets protected by PT_DENY_ATTACH + Hardened Runtime + SIP
Apple Silicon hardware detection queries macOS system APIs including sysctl and system_profiler for chip family, memory, and GPU cores
Model management scans HuggingFace cache for MLX models and filters by available memory with SHA-256 weight fingerprinting for integrity
launchd integration provides user agent management with explicit user control (no auto-start or auto-restart) when user starts via CLI
Hypervisor memory isolation uses Apple's Hypervisor.framework with Stage 2 page tables to make inference memory invisible to RDMA
Wire protocol defines JSON message types over WebSocket including Register, Heartbeat, InferenceRequest, AttestationChallenge with encrypted payloads
External dependencies include tokio for async runtime, reqwest for HTTP, crypto_box for NaCl encryption, pyo3 for Python integration, and security-framework for macOS
Service creates ephemeral E2E encryption key pair and Secure Enclave signing handle for attestation at startup
Inference request flow includes coordinator message reception, decryption, InferenceAccepted response, and backend routing
AtomicProviderStats provides thread-safe counters for requests served and tokens generated shared between event loop and heartbeats
Health monitoring uses 15-second intervals with 5 consecutive failures triggering backend restart using PID-targeted SIGTERM/SIGKILL
Device attestation enrollment requests per-device ACME profile from coordinator and integrates with macOS System Settings
Model catalog fetching from coordinator with fallback to hardcoded catalog when coordinator is unreachable

decrypt-test.claim: The component has minimal external dependencies: crypto_box v0.9 for NaCl cryptography and base64 v0.22 for encoding
The component is designed for cross-language compatibility testing with specific usage for NaCl Box decryption
Main entry point processes command-line arguments and validates argument count
Base64 decoding layer processes all three input parameters with error handling
Input validation ensures 32-byte keys and minimum 24-byte ciphertext for nonce
Cryptographic operations use X25519 key exchange and XSalsa20-Poly1305 AEAD for NaCl Box decryption
Output handler converts decrypted bytes to UTF-8 and prints to stdout
Uses crypto_box crate for NaCl cryptographic primitives and base64 crate for encoding
Extracts 24-byte nonce from the beginning of ciphertext as part of the decryption flow
CLI interface expects three base64-encoded arguments: ephemeral public key, ciphertext, and provider private key

verify-attestation.claim: The main function reads attestation JSON from a fixed path and handles file read errors
Calls attestation.VerifyJSON() to perform verification and handles parsing errors
Outputs hardware information and provides clear success/failure indication
Imports the coordinator's internal attestation package
VerifyJSON function provides the core verification logic used by the CLI
AttestationBlob struct ensures JSON compatibility between Swift and Go
Comprehensive verification process including signature verification and security checks
Package documentation describes cross-language verification between Swift and Go
Security requirements validation including Secure Enclave, SIP, and Secure Boot checks
Cross-language verification success message indicates Swift to Go compatibility

web.claim: The component uses Next.js 16, React 19, and key dependencies including Privy for authentication, TweetNaCl for encryption, and Zustand for state management
Root layout orchestrates multiple providers including authentication (Privy), theming, verification mode, and analytics integrations
Main chat page implements comprehensive state management with streaming AI conversations, model selection, and authentication handling
All requests go through Next.js API routes to avoid CORS, with API keys passed as custom headers for coordinator forwarding
Chat streaming supports optional end-to-end encryption using NaCl Box sealing to coordinator's X25519 public key
Zustand store manages chat state with persistence, including chat creation, message management, and model selection
Encryption system implements X25519 NaCl Box with ephemeral keypairs for forward secrecy in coordinator requests
Authentication hook manages Privy integration and automatic API key provisioning using access tokens
AppShell provides main application layout with sidebar navigation and special handling for device linking flow
Chat API route acts as proxy to coordinator, handling both plaintext and sealed request forwarding with streaming support
Auth keys endpoint provisions coordinator API keys using Privy authorization headers or cookie tokens
Chat message component supports thinking processes with expandable UI and markdown rendering for rich content
Provider dashboard fetches machine status with 15-second refresh intervals and comprehensive error handling
Component implements comprehensive design system with EigenCloud brand palette and custom font loading
Next.js configuration handles TypeScript build issues with noble/curves dependency and enables type checking bypasses
Testing setup uses Vitest with jsdom environment, global test utilities, and path alias resolution
Test suite covers API functions with mocked fetch responses and localStorage simulation
Sidebar provides navigation with chat history, provider dashboard, billing, and API console access
TrustMetadata interface captures hardware attestation information including Secure Enclave verification and device details
Stripe integration provides payout status, onboarding flows, and withdrawal processing for provider earnings
